constat.heading: Why so little participation in the country's decisions?
constat.stat1Label: abstention rate in the 2022 legislative elections
constat.stat2Label: of French people believe politicians are corrupt
constat.stat3Label: of registered voters did not vote in any round
constat.problem1Desc: Assemblée Nationale, Senate, Légifrance, Official Journal… everything is spread out and impossible to find for the average citizen.
constat.problem2Desc: Written for lawyers, not everyday citizens. Understanding it requires real effort.
constat.problem3Desc: Dozens of texts voted on every month. Impossible to keep up — most people eventually give up.

whatIs.heading: What is LoiClair?
whatIs.para1: LoiClair is a website that makes French law and politics genuinely accessible to everyone. We allow anyone to translate and simplify legislative texts, amendments, parliamentary debates and institutional processes into clear, concrete, relatable explanations — powered by artificial intelligence. We also produce statistics directly from official institutional data.
whatIs.para2: Our goal: to answer the real questions citizens have ('But who actually decides?', 'Will this change my daily life?') by explaining the actual steps involved (often very different from the theory), the key players, and the day-to-day impacts.

howBuilt.heading: How the site was built
howBuilt.para1: LoiClair is an independent project made possible through the use of
howBuilt.para1Suffix: : from writing the code to analysing and structuring raw data, including intelligent summaries of legislative texts and parliamentary debates.
howBuilt.tech3Desc: hosted PostgreSQL database storing all legislative data
howBuilt.tech4Desc: hosting and automatic deployment on every code update
howBuilt.tech5Desc: daily automated import of data from official sources

sources.heading: Our Official Sources
sources.intro: All information comes directly from institutions, without modification or partisan interpretation. Automatically updated via API/open data.
sources.source1Desc: Legislative dossiers, MPs, amendments, votes, proceedings
sources.source5Desc: Full legislative texts, consolidated versions of legal codes, implementation decrees (via PISTE / DILA API)
sources.source6Desc: Tracking the implementation of enacted laws: implementation rates, expected measures, published decrees (Assemblée Nationale)

feedback.heading: Share your feedback and contribute!
feedback.desc: LoiClair lives thanks to you. Feedback, feature ideas, laws to prioritise, error reports — all contributions are welcome, technical or not.
feedback.license: Open source project under AGPL v3 licence

process.pageDesc: Understanding how laws are born: from the institutions involved to the full journey of a legislative text.

organes.heading: The Main Institutions and Their Roles

assemblee.content: The Assemblée Nationale is the lower house of Parliament, made up of 577 MPs elected by direct universal suffrage for 5 years. Its main role is to vote on laws, scrutinise the Government and debate public policy. It has the final say in the event of disagreement with the Senate on most legislation.

senat.content: The Senate is the upper house, with 348 senators elected by indirect suffrage for 6 years. It represents the territories (regions, departments). It examines laws after the Assemblée Nationale, proposes amendments, and balances national and local interests. It cannot be dissolved.

gouvernement.content: Led by the Prime Minister, the Government initiates the majority of bills (around 90%). It enforces laws, manages the administration, and can stake its responsibility on a text (article 49-3). It is accountable to the Assemblée Nationale.

president.content: The President promulgates laws passed by Parliament. They can request a second reading or refer a law to the Constitutional Council. They appoint the Prime Minister and can dissolve the Assemblée Nationale.

conseil.content: Made up of 9 members, it verifies the constitutionality of laws before promulgation (referred by the President, Prime Minister, speakers of the assemblies, or 60 members of Parliament). It protects fundamental rights and the balance of powers.

parcours.heading: A law's journey
parcours.finalNote: Average duration: 6 to 12 months • Fast-track procedures possible (article 49-3, emergency…)
parcours.step1Details: The text is filed either by the Government (bill) or by a member of Parliament (private member's bill).
parcours.step2Details: Detailed study, hearings, rapporteur appointed, proposed changes.
parcours.step3Desc: Debate and vote in the chamber
parcours.step3Details: Discussion article by article, then a vote on the whole text.
parcours.step4Details: Back-and-forth exchanges or compromise in the Joint Committee (Commission Mixte Paritaire).
parcours.step5Desc: Last word to the Assemblée Nationale
parcours.step5Details: The Assemblée Nationale makes the final decision (except in special cases).
parcours.step6Details: Verification of compliance with the Constitution.
parcours.step7Details: The President signs and publishes the law in the Official Journal.
parcours.step8Details: The Government publishes the decrees needed for the law to be effectively applied. Without these decrees, the law remains unenforceable.

textTypes.pageTitle: The Different Types of Legislative Texts
textTypes.pageDesc: Understanding the different categories of texts produced by Parliament and the Government.
textTypes.sectionParlementaireDesc: Texts initiated or produced by Parliament (National Assembly / Senate)
textTypes.sectionGouvernementalDesc: Texts initiated by the Government or executive branch

propositionLoi.content: Initiated by a member of Parliament (MP or senator). Often on specific topics, such as local or thematic legislation.

propositionLoiOrganique.trigger: Organic Private Member's Bill (Proposition de Loi Organique)
propositionLoiOrganique.content: A very specific variant aimed at amending or supplementing organic laws (texts that specify how the Constitution is applied).
propositionLoiOrganique.reinforced1: Absolute majority in each assembly (289 votes in the Assemblée Nationale, 174 in the Senate)
propositionLoiOrganique.reinforced2: Senate has equal power (no exclusive last word)
propositionLoiOrganique.reinforced3: Mandatory review by the Constitutional Council
propositionLoiOrganique.reinforced4: 15-day waiting period before examination in plenary session
propositionLoiOrganique.examples: Examples: rules of procedure of the assemblies, status of members of Parliament.

amendement.content: A modification proposed by a member of Parliament or the Government on a text under discussion. A bill can receive hundreds of amendments before its final vote.

rapport.content: An analytical document produced by a parliamentary committee or rapporteur.

resolution.content: A non-binding text expressing a position (e.g., a European resolution).

projetLoi.content: Initiated by the Government. Examples: budgets (PLF), social reforms. It follows the full legislative procedure.

ordonnance.content: A measure taken by the Government, with Parliament's authorization (Article 38 of the Constitution), in a domain normally reserved for law. It must be ratified by Parliament to have permanent legal force. Example: labor code reform in 2017.

decret.content: A regulatory text issued by the President of the Republic or the Prime Minister to specify the implementation details of a law. It does not go through Parliament. Examples: implementation decree, appointment decree.

landing.heroTitle: Every month, laws are passed <highlight>in your name.</highlight>
landing.heroSubtitle: Do you know what has been decided in your name lately?
landing.heroDescription: LoiClair translates French legislative activity into plain language — to understand, follow, and participate.
landing.hero2Title: With LoiClair, French politics is <highlight>finally readable.</highlight>
landing.hero2Description: A civic toolbox that turns legislative jargon into plain language. Key statistics on parliamentary activity, a simplified view of every dossier, a news feed to follow day by day everything happening in Parliament: new bills, decisions, votes…
landing.feedTitle3: Law no. 2026-142 on the protection of personal data
landing.dossierItem2Title: Bill on the right to housing
landing.dossierDescription: A legislative dossier is the complete journey of a bill, from filing to final vote. Search, filter and track every bill or proposition. Status, chamber, AI summary — all there, up to date.
landing.feedSectionTitle: Follow daily parliamentary activity, <highlight>step by step.</highlight>
landing.feedDescription: As simple as a social media feed. Every month, LoiClair gathers filed bills, floor votes and enacted laws into a clear, chronological feed. No more hunting for information.
landing.dashboardDescription: Assembly composition, group breakdown, gender parity, average age — Parliament's key figures at a glance.
landing.beforeAfterTitle: AI summarises every bill for you
landing.beforeAfterDescription: No more deciphering legal jargon. Our AI analyses and summarises each legislative dossier in plain language, in seconds.
landing.beforeLine1: Article L. 432-1 of the Civil Code — Pursuant to Decree no. 2024-1138
landing.beforeLine3: relating to the terms of application of paragraph 3
landing.beforeLine4: in accordance with the provisions of Order no. 2023-389
landing.beforeLine5: of 12 May 2023 on the exercise of powers of...
landing.beforeLine6: local authorities in matters of public management
landing.beforeLine7: ...referred to above, after opinion of the Council of State...
landing.beforeLine8: § 4 — The cumulative conditions defined in Art. 7
landing.beforeLine9: must be assessed on a case-by-case basis...
landing.beforeLine10: taking into account the circumstances referred to in Art. 12 bis
landing.afterDescription: This text sets the State budget for 2026. It determines how much the State can spend and where the money comes from (taxes, borrowing…). Each ministry receives an allocation.
landing.afterBullet2: Presented by the government in September
landing.afterBullet3: Must be voted on before 31 December
landing.featuresDescription: Everything you need to understand, follow and engage with what is happening in Parliament.
landing.feature2Description: Every bill translated into everyday language, jargon-free.
landing.feature3Description: Gender parity, political groups, votes — all in numbers.
landing.feature4Description: Locate your MP or Senator in seconds.
landing.feature5Description: Share your opinion and compare it to Parliament's votes.
landing.feature6Description: The essentials of parliamentary activity each month.
landing.feature7Description: Follow a bill or a representative, get notified.
landing.feature8Description: Every week, parliamentary activity summarised by email.
landing.ctaTitle: What if politics were finally readable?
landing.ctaDescription: Understanding is the first step. Following is taking back control. Participating is changing things.
landing.ctaFootnote: Private beta — Official data from the Assemblée nationale

dossiers.pageDesc: Bills are organised by legislative dossiers. A legislative dossier is the complete journey of a bill or government proposal, from its filing to its enactment (or withdrawal).
dossiers.dataSource: Data from the 17th legislature, updated daily, sourced from
dossiers.noResults: No legislative dossier found with these filters.

kpis.filterTypeTooltipDesc: E.g. ordinary legislative procedure, finance bill, etc.
kpis.filterGroupeTooltipDesc: E.g. La France Insoumise, Renaissance, Les Républicains...
kpis.funnelStep3Desc: At least one chamber voted in favour
kpis.funnelStep4Desc: Signed by the President of the Republic
kpis.funnelStep5Desc: Law in force: decrees published or directly applicable
kpis.funnelDesc: From filing to implementation: how dossiers progress through the legislative process
kpis.statusEnTraitementDesc: The text has been filed in Parliament (Assemblée Nationale or Senate) and is being examined for a decision
kpis.statusAdoptesANDesc: The Assemblée Nationale has adopted it. Next step: examination by the Senate
kpis.statusAdoptesSNDesc: The Senate has adopted it. Next step: examination by the Assemblée Nationale
kpis.statusAdoptesParlemDesc: Both chambers have adopted the text. Next step: enactment
kpis.statusPromulguesDesc: The President has signed the law into force. Published in the Official Journal
kpis.statusAppliquéesDesc: Law in force: directly applicable or implementing decrees published
kpis.statusRejétésDesc: The text has been abandoned (unless re-filed or revised)
kpis.tauxPromulgationDesc: Share of completed dossiers that resulted in a law signed by the President
kpis.tauxApplicationDesc: Share of legislative-scope texts that are effectively implemented (decrees published or directly applicable)
kpis.delaiApplicationDesc: Average time to publish all implementing decrees for a law
kpis.delaiPromulgationDesc: Average time between filing and enactment of a law
kpis.delaiPromulgationSubtitle: From filing to the Official Journal
kpis.delaiANTitle: Average delay in the Assemblée Nationale
kpis.delaiANDesc: From filing at the AN to the adoption decision by the Assemblée Nationale
kpis.delaiANSubtitle: Per text adopted by the AN
kpis.delaiSNDesc: From filing at the Senate to the adoption decision by the Senate
kpis.delaiSNSubtitle: Per text adopted by the Senate
kpis.textesProposesDDesc: Number of legislative dossiers filed by political group
kpis.loisPromulgueesDesc: Number of laws effectively enacted by political group
kpis.successLegislatifDesc: Adoption by the Assemblée Nationale and Senate: across all texts relevant to both chambers. Enactment: across legislative-scope texts only (some texts examined by Parliament are not intended to be enacted, such as resolutions).
kpis.parlementairesDesc: Ranking of the 20 most involved MPs and senators in legislative texts, as authors, co-authors, or rapporteurs.

month.pageDescription: Monthly legislative events: votes, filings, enactments and more.

ccSaisine.AN: 60 or more MPs referred the case to the Constitutional Council.
ccSaisine.SN: 60 or more senators referred the case to the Constitutional Council.
ccSaisine.PM: The Prime Minister referred the case to the Constitutional Council.
ccSaisine.auto: Automatic referral (organic law or finance law).

month.noEventsOfType: No events of this type in {month}.
month.noEventsRecorded: No legislative events recorded. Try another month.

adopteAN.definition: The National Assembly has voted on the text. The Senate must still weigh in.

adopteParlement.definition: Both chambers have voted on the text in identical terms. It now awaits promulgation by the President.

adopteSenat.definition: The Senate has voted on the text. The National Assembly must still weigh in.

enCoursExamen.definition: The text is still being debated in Parliament. It has not yet been definitively adopted.

promulguee.definition: The text has been signed by the President and published in the Official Journal. It has the force of law.

rejete.definition: The text was rejected by one or both chambers. A new filing remains possible.

allocutionPresident.term: Address by the President of the National Assembly
allocutionPresident.definition: An official speech delivered by the Speaker of the National Assembly during a session. This is not a legislative text.

commissionEnquete.definition: A parliamentary inquiry into a matter of public interest, conducted by a group of MPs. It produces a report, not a law.

engagementResponsabilite.definition: A procedure by which the Prime Minister puts the government's survival on the line before the National Assembly (article 49 of the Constitution). It can result in a text being adopted without a vote, or the government being brought down.

missionInformation.definition: A parliamentary working group tasked with studying a specific topic and producing a report. It has no binding power.

petitions.definition: A request addressed by citizens to Parliament on a matter of general interest. It is examined by a committee but does not produce a law.

projetFinancementSecu.definition: The annual Social Security budget, filed by the government each autumn. It sets spending and revenue targets for the following year.

projetFinancesAnnee.definition: The annual State budget, filed by the government each autumn. It authorises spending and revenue for the following year.

projetFinancesRectificative.definition: A mid-year revision to the State budget. It adjusts the initial forecasts based on economic conditions.

projetLoiOrdinaire.definition: A text prepared and filed by the government, then submitted for a parliamentary vote. It is the most common type of legislative text.

projetRatificationTraites.definition: Parliamentary authorisation for the President to ratify an international treaty. Parliament cannot amend the treaty text itself.

projetLoiConstitutionnelle.definition: A text aiming to amend the Constitution. Its adoption requires a special procedure (referendum or Congress vote).

projetLoiOrganique.definition: A text specifying how the Constitution is applied in practice. Its adoption follows a reinforced procedure with mandatory review by the Constitutional Council.

propositionLoiOrdinaire.definition: A text filed by one or more members of Parliament (MP or senator). Unlike a government bill, it does not originate from the executive.

rapportInformation.definition: A report produced by one or more members of Parliament on a topic of their choosing. It informs Parliament but has no binding value.

resolution.definition: A text expressing a position of Parliament, with no legislative value. It creates neither obligation nor right.

resolutionArticle34.definition: A resolution passed under article 34-1 of the Constitution, allowing Parliament to express a wish or a policy direction. It has no force of law.

responsabilitePenale.term: Criminal liability of the President of the Republic
responsabilitePenale.definition: An exceptional constitutional procedure to initiate criminal proceedings against the head of state. It goes through the High Court.

premierDepot.definition: The first step: the author (government or MP) formally files their text with Parliament. This marks the start of the legislative procedure.

premiereRecture1.definition: The first full examination of the text by the chamber that received it first. It includes committee work followed by a plenary debate.

premiereRecture2.definition: The first examination of the text by the second chamber, after adoption by the first. The parliamentary shuttle begins.

accordInternational.definition: A treaty or international convention linked to the dossier. Parliament authorises ratification but cannot amend the treaty text.

avisConseilEtat.definition: An advisory opinion issued by the Council of State on a government bill before it is filed with Parliament. It is mandatory for all government bills.

commissionMixteParity.definition: A meeting of 7 MPs and 7 senators tasked with finding a compromise text when both chambers disagree. The result is then put to a vote in both assemblies.

conclusionConseilConstitutionnel.definition: The final decision of the Constitutional Council on whether the text complies with the Constitution. It may validate, partially strike down, or fully strike down the text.

conseilConstitutionnel.definition: The stage where the text is reviewed for constitutional compliance. The Council verifies that the law respects the Constitution.

convocationCMP.definition: The Prime Minister (or the speakers of both assemblies for a private member's bill) requests the convening of a Joint Committee. This marks the start of the conciliation procedure.

creationCommissionEnquete.term: Creation of a committee of inquiry
creationCommissionEnquete.definition: The National Assembly's decision to launch a parliamentary inquiry. The committee has extensive investigative powers.

creationMissionInformation.definition: The decision to create a parliamentary working group on a given topic. The mission will produce a report at the end of its work.

debat.definition: A discussion in plenary session on the text or a related topic. MPs set out their positions and examine amendments.

decision.definition: The chamber's final vote on the text in plenary session. The text is adopted, rejected, or adopted with modifications.

decisionCMP.definition: The result of the Joint Committee's work. It can lead to an agreement (shared text) or a failure (deadlock).

decisionMotionCensure.term: Vote on a motion of no confidence
decisionMotionCensure.definition: The National Assembly's vote on a motion of no confidence against the government. If passed, the government falls.

depotProjetLoi.definition: The government formally files a text with Parliament. It is accompanied by an impact assessment and the Council of State's opinion.

depotDeclarationGouvernement.definition: The government submits an official declaration to Parliament, which may give rise to a debate. It has no legislative value.

depotInitiativeNavette.term: Filing of an initiative in the shuttle
depotInitiativeNavette.definition: Transmission of the text from one chamber to the other after a first vote. The shuttle continues until both chambers adopt identical text.

depotLettreRectificative.definition: The government amends its own bill before or during examination. This allows the text to be updated without withdrawing it.

depotRapport.definition: The rapporteur submits the committee report to the assembly. This document summarises the committee's work and recommendations.

depotRapportCMP.term: Filing of a Joint Committee report
depotRapportCMP.definition: Publication of the compromise text produced by the Joint Committee. It will be put to a vote in both chambers.

deuxiemeLecture.definition: A second examination of the text by a chamber, after the other chamber has made changes. The debate focuses only on the remaining points of disagreement.

discussionSeancePublique.definition: Examination of the text by all members of Parliament in the chamber. This is where amendments are debated and voted on.

etudeImpact.definition: A mandatory document accompanying government bills, assessing the economic, social and legal consequences of the text. It is published before examination begins.

procedureAcceleree.definition: The government requests a fast examination of the text, limiting the shuttle to a single reading per chamber. This significantly speeds up the process.

lectureDefinitive.definition: The National Assembly's last vote when the Joint Committee has failed and the government gives it the final say. The Senate can no longer oppose it.

lectureUnique.definition: The text is examined in a single reading by one chamber, with no possibility of a shuttle. This applies to certain resolutions or special texts.

miseEnApplication.definition: Publication of the decrees and orders needed for the law to take concrete effect. Without these texts, some provisions remain unenforceable.

motionCensure.definition: A procedure by which the National Assembly can bring down the government. It requires the signature of at least one tenth of MPs to be filed.

motionProcedure.definition: A request made during a session to change the course of a text's examination. It may aim to reject the text without debate or refer it back to committee.

nominationRapporteur.definition: The committee designates one of its members to study the text in detail and write a report. The rapporteur becomes the key reference for the text in committee and in plenary.

nominationRapporteurBudgetaire.definition: The committee designates a specialist rapporteur to examine a part of the budget. Each budget rapporteur covers a specific policy area (defence, education, health, etc.).

nouvelleLecture.definition: Resumption of the examination of the text by one chamber after the Joint Committee has failed. Both chambers try to agree on a common text.

promulgationLoi1.definition: The President of the Republic signs the law and orders its publication in the Official Journal. The law takes effect the day after publication, unless stated otherwise.

promulgationLoi2.definition: The President of the Republic signs the law and orders its publication in the Official Journal. The law takes effect the day after publication, unless stated otherwise.

rapportApplicationLois.term: Report on the implementation of laws
rapportApplicationLois.definition: An assessment drawn up by Parliament on the effective implementation of a passed law. It checks whether the implementing decrees have been published.

renvoiCommissionFond.definition: The text is referred to the main committee responsible for examining it. This committee will write the report and propose amendments.

renvoiPrealable.definition: A procedure by which the text is sent back to committee before being debated in plenary. This allows for thorough examination before the public debate.

renvoiPrealableCAE.term: Prior referral to the Foreign Affairs Committee
renvoiPrealableCAE.definition: The text is referred to the Foreign Affairs Committee for examination. This applies notably to treaties and international conventions.

retraitInitiative.definition: The author of the text (government or MP) withdraws their own bill or proposal. The dossier is closed.

reunionCommission.definition: A working session of the parliamentary committee tasked with examining the text. Amendments are debated and voted on before the plenary debate.

saisineConseilConstitutionnel.definition: MPs, the President or the Prime Minister ask the Constitutional Council to verify the text's compliance with the Constitution. This step can occur before promulgation.

saisiePourAvisCommission.definition: A committee is consulted to give its opinion on a text that falls mainly within the remit of another committee. Its opinion is advisory.

travaux.definition: The set of activities related to the examination of a dossier. This generic term covers meetings, hearings and discussions.

travauxCommissionSaisieAvis.definition: Work carried out by a committee consulted for an advisory opinion on a text. It may propose amendments but the final decision rests with the lead committee.

travauxCommissionFond.definition: Work of the main committee responsible for examining the text. It writes the report and amendments submitted to the full assembly.

travauxCommissions.definition: The phase where the text is examined by parliamentary committees. Committee members hear experts, debate and propose changes.

texteDepose.definition: The initial version of the text, as filed by its author (MP or government). This marks the start of the procedure.

commission.definition: The version of the text amended by the parliamentary committee responsible for examining it. It incorporates the amendments adopted in committee.

seance.definition: The version of the text adopted in plenary session, after debates and amendments in the chamber. This is the text as voted by the chamber.

legifrance.definition: The official version published in the Official Journal after promulgation. This is the definitive law text, as referenced on Légifrance.

docs.guideDesc: How to read and use LoiClair's dashboards, indicators and legislative dossiers.
docs.methodeDesc: How indicators are calculated, where the data comes from, and what their limitations are.

resumeIA.noTextAvailable: No text available for this dossier.
resumeIA.textNotAvailable: This text is not yet available online. Change version below.
resumeIA.consolidatedOnLegifrance: The consolidated version can be viewed directly on Légifrance.
resumeIA.aiSummaryComingSoon: AI summary of this version will be available soon.
resumeIA.generationFailed: The summary could not be generated.
resumeIA.perplexityPromptFr: Analyse et explique ce texte législatif français pour en discuter avec moi : "{title}". Voici le lien officiel : {link}. Résume les points clés, les objectifs, les impacts concrets et le contexte politique.
resumeIA.perplexityPromptEn: Analyze and explain this French legislative text so we can discuss it: "{title}". Here is the official link: {link}. Summarize the key points, objectives, concrete impacts and political context.

errors.notFoundTitle: This page does not exist <highlight>in our archives.</highlight>
errors.notFoundSubtitle: The link may be invalid, or the page has been moved.
errors.notFoundJoke: It was probably sent back to committee.
errors.notFoundHelp: Find legislative dossiers, the news feed and key indicators on LoiClair.
errors.errorSubtitle: An error occurred while connecting to the database. Check your connection and try again.
errors.errorJoke: The database is probably in committee too.
